title: Incursa.Quic Standard Client TLS Validation Work Item
summary: Implement the narrowest honest standard public client-validation mode on the existing `SslClientAuthenticationOptions` carrier while keeping the exact-pinning floor on `QuicPeerCertificatePolicy` separate.
planned_changes:
- Validate the mainstream client path with the existing `SslClientAuthenticationOptions` carrier, including `TargetHost`, `CertificateChainPolicy`, `CertificateRevocationCheckMode`, and `RemoteCertificateValidationCallback`.
- Thread the captured standard validation inputs through the client options validator, client host, runtime, and TLS bridge driver without introducing a new QUIC-specific carrier.
- Keep `QuicClientConnectionOptions.PeerCertificatePolicy` and `QuicPeerCertificatePolicy` as the separate exact-pinning floor.
- Reject mixed-mode configuration so the exact-pinning and standard validation semantics cannot be conflated.
- Add focused requirement-home proof for standard validation success, trust mismatch, hostname mismatch, mixed-mode rejection, and the unchanged exact-pinning floor.
verification_plan: Run focused requirement-home tests that prove standard BCL-shaped validation success, trust mismatch failure, hostname mismatch failure, mixed-mode rejection, and the unchanged exact-pinning floor. Keep the incumbent `REQ-QUIC-API-0005`, `REQ-QUIC-API-0012`, and `REQ-QUIC-CRT-0123` coverage green, then run the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep and the repo-local SpecTrace render/check flow if canonical JSON changes.
completion_notes: This work item now covers the mainstream standard validation path on the existing client carrier plus the exact-pinning coexistence boundary. It does not introduce a new QUIC-specific validation object.
